scope: C-05 code-mode exec/wait runtime, tool definitions, response adaptation, and turn-host nested-tool dispatch

foldedSourceFiles.reason: AgenC keeps the QuickJS runtime lifecycle separate from the turn-scoped nested-tool host.
AgenC tool handlers are plain Tool objects; exec argument parsing and service invocation live in the exec Tool implementation.
AgenC wait argument parsing and service invocation live in the wait Tool implementation.
AgenC splits pragma parsing, adapter lifecycle, and nested dispatch coverage across focused TypeScript tests.
AgenC maps freeform ToolSpec construction to its Tool interface and registry raw-string argument handling.

requiredBehaviors: exec allocates a cell, parses an optional first-line pragma, starts isolated JavaScript execution, and returns yielded or terminal output
wait resumes, collects, or terminates yielded cells and reports missing cells as model-visible errors
stored values survive across exec cells in the same service
reject exec/wait self-invocation from nested code-mode tools
dispatch explicitly idempotent read-only enabled non-exec nested tools through the registry code-mode path
fail closed for side-effecting, non-idempotent, control/discovery, or approval-required nested tools when no permission-aware nested runtime hook is available
support JSON-object inputs and string inputs for string-argument nested tools
propagate nested-tool cancellation signals into tool execution arguments
return the code-mode result projection when present
emit non-blank code-mode notify output as turn progress events
derive code-mode-safe JavaScript identifiers for enabled nested tools
build exec and wait descriptions with nested-tool metadata
render runtime responses with script status, elapsed time, output items, error text, and token-bounded text output
